Title: User Interaction with Map on App Launch
Preconditions: App is installed on an Android device (version 8.1 or higher)
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app for the first time.
2. Observe the default view.
Expected Result: The app automatically redirects the user to a map interface displaying air quality data for the user’s current location.

Title: Text Search for Specific Location
Preconditions: App is installed and launched
Test Data: A known city or address (e.g., "Venice, Italy")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the search bar on the map interface.
2. Enter the test location (e.g., "Venice, Italy") into the search bar.
3. Press the search or enter key.
Expected Result: The map centers on the searched location, and air quality data for that location is displayed.

Title: Applying Filters on Map
Preconditions: App is installed and launched
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Open the map interface.
2. Locate and select the filter option (e.g., AQI range, PM2.5 levels, or other available filters).
3. Apply a specific filter (e.g., "High AQI areas").
Expected Result: The map updates to display only locations or areas matching the selected filter criteria.

Title: Real-Time Air Quality Data Retrieval via AirVisual API
Preconditions: App is connected to the internet; AirVisual API is accessible
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app and ensure the map is displayed.
2. Allow the app to access GPS location (if prompted).
3. Wait for data to load.
Expected Result: The app retrieves and displays real-time AQI, PM2.5 concentrations, temperature, and humidity data for the user’s current location.

Title: Real-Time Weather Data Retrieval via OpenWeatherMap API
Preconditions: App is connected to the internet; OpenWeatherMap API is accessible
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app and navigate to a location with weather data available.
2. Ensure the app has permission to access location (if required).
3. Wait for data to load.
Expected Result: The app retrieves and displays real-time weather data (e.g., temperature, humidity) for the selected location.

Title: App Launch on Android Device (Version 8.1 or Higher)
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android device with version 8.1 or higher
Test Steps:
1. Install the app on the test device.
2. Launch the app.
Expected Result: The app installs and launches successfully without errors on the specified Android version.

Title: App Background Execution for Notifications
Preconditions: App is installed; user has enabled notifications
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app and navigate to settings.
2. Enable notification preferences for air quality alerts.
3. Simulate a change in air quality (e.g., AQI threshold breach) via API.
Expected Result: The app sends a notification to the user’s device alerting them about the change in air quality.

Title: Receive Air Quality Notifications
Preconditions: User has granted permission to the app to send notifications
Test Data: AQI value exceeding predefined safety threshold (e.g., 100)
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app and ensures notification permissions are enabled.
2. System detects AQI value exceeding the threshold (e.g., 100).
3. App sends a push notification to the user’s device.
Expected Result: User receives a notification alerting them of the air quality change.

Title: Notification Triggered by Data Change
Preconditions: User has enabled notifications and is logged into the app
Test Data: PM2.5 level exceeds safety threshold (e.g., 50 µg/m³)
Test Steps:
1. User selects a location in the app.
2. System monitors PM2.5 levels in real-time.
3. PM2.5 level exceeds the predefined threshold.
4. App triggers a notification.
Expected Result: User receives a notification indicating the PM2.5 level breach.

Title: View Practical Emission Reduction Tips
Preconditions: App is installed on the user’s device
Test Data: None
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app and selects the "Suggerimenti pratici" (Practical Tips) icon.
2. App loads the tips section.
Expected Result: User sees a list of suggestions for reducing atmospheric emissions.

Title: Add Air Quality Widget to Home Screen
Preconditions: App is installed on the user’s device
Test Data: User’s preferred location (e.g., "Roma")
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app and navigates to widget settings.
2. User selects the air quality widget option.
3. User chooses a location (e.g., "Roma").
4. User adds the widget to the home screen.
Expected Result: Widget appears on the home screen displaying real-time AQI data for the selected location.

Title: Widget Displays Correct Data
Preconditions: Widget is added to the home screen with a selected location
Test Data: AQI value for the selected location (e.g., 75)
Test Steps:
1. User checks the widget on the home screen.
2. System updates AQI data in real-time.
Expected Result: Widget displays the current AQI value for the selected location.

Title: Real-Time AQI Visualization
Preconditions: User has selected a location (GPS or manual)
Test Data: AQI value for the selected location (e.g., 60)
Test Steps:
1. User opens the app and selects a location (GPS or manual).
2. App fetches real-time AQI data from the API.
3. App displays AQI value on the map or dashboard.
Expected Result: AQI value is displayed accurately for the selected location.

Title: Automatic Data Update
Preconditions: User is viewing AQI data for a location
Test Data: Updated AQI value (e.g., 80)
Test Steps:
1. User views AQI data on the app.
2. System fetches updated AQI data from the API.
3. App refreshes the displayed AQI value automatically.
Expected Result: AQI value updates in real-time without manual refresh.

Title: Secure API Communication and Error Handling
Preconditions: Application is installed and connected to an API
Test Data: Simulated API failure (timeout, invalid response, server error)
Test Steps:
1. Trigger an API request (e.g., fetch air quality data).
2. Simulate an API error (e.g., timeout after 10 seconds).
3. Observe system behavior during and after the error.
Expected Result: The application handles the exception gracefully without crashing, displays an appropriate error message to the user, and maintains data integrity.

Title: Response Time for User Interactions
Preconditions: Application is running on a smartphone/tablet with stable internet
Test Data: User actions (e.g., button press, data refresh)
Test Steps:
1. Measure time taken for a user interaction (e.g., tapping a button to refresh data).
2. Measure time taken for data retrieval from API and display on screen.
Expected Result: The application responds to user interactions in <2 seconds and displays retrieved data within <5 seconds.

Title: Cross-Device Compatibility (Smartphone/Tablet)
Preconditions: Application installed on at least 3 different devices (1 smartphone, 2 tablets)
Test Data: Standard user workflow (e.g., login, data retrieval, map interaction)
Test Steps:
1. Perform a predefined workflow on each device.
2. Verify all UI elements are correctly rendered and functional.
3. Check for layout inconsistencies or missing features.
Expected Result: The application functions identically across all devices with no loss of features or usability issues.

Title: Clean and Responsive UI Design
Preconditions: Application installed on a smartphone/tablet
Test Data: UI elements (buttons, menus, data displays)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate through all main screens and interactions.
2. Verify UI elements are uncluttered and follow responsive design principles.
3. Check for visual errors (e.g., overlapping elements, misaligned text).
Expected Result: The interface is clean, intuitive, and free of visual errors on all supported devices.

Title: Data Deletion Upon Uninstallation
Preconditions: Application installed and user data created (e.g., saved preferences, cached API responses)
Test Data: None (system-generated data)
Test Steps:
1. Uninstall the application from the device.
2. Check device storage for residual application data.
Expected Result: No application-related data (e.g., caches, preferences) remains on the device after uninstallation.

Title: Mandatory Internet Connection for Functionality
Preconditions: Application installed on a device with GPS and internet toggles
Test Data: Disabled internet connection
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to use a feature requiring internet (e.g., fetch air quality data).
2. Observe system behavior when no internet is available.
Expected Result: The application displays a clear error message indicating the need for an internet connection and disables affected features.

Title: GPS Dependency for User-Specific Data
Preconditions: Application installed on a device with GPS enabled/disabled
Test Data: User location data (simulated or real)
Test Steps:
1. Enable GPS and request location-based data (e.g., nearest air monitoring station).
2. Disable GPS and repeat the request.
Expected Result: The feature works correctly with GPS enabled. With GPS disabled, the application either prompts the user to enable it or displays a fallback message (if applicable).

Title: Handling Concurrent API Requests
Preconditions: Application connected to API with stable network
Test Data: Two simultaneous API requests (e.g., fetch data for two different locations)
Test Steps:
1. Initiate two concurrent API requests (e.g., via rapid button presses or automated script).
2. Monitor system response and data integrity.
Expected Result: The application processes both requests without crashes, timeouts, or data loss. Responses are delivered in full.

Title: Data Recovery After Connection Loss
Preconditions: Application connected to API with active session
Test Data: Simulated network disconnection/reconnection
Test Steps:
1. Perform an API request while the connection is stable.
2. Disconnect from the internet during data retrieval.
3. Reconnect and observe system behavior.
Expected Result: Upon reconnection, the application automatically retrieves pending data or resumes the interrupted operation without data loss.

Title: Enable Notifications Functionality
Preconditions: User has not activated notifications previously
Test Data: Valid user account, notification settings page accessible
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. User navigates to the notification settings section.
3. User selects the option to enable notifications.
Expected Result: Notifications are activated, and the user can receive data without opening the app.

Title: Add Widget to Home Screen
Preconditions: User has not added any widget previously
Test Data: Valid user account, home screen accessible, widget list available
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application.
2. User navigates to the widget selection menu.
3. User selects a widget from the available options.
4. User confirms the addition of the widget to the home screen.
Expected Result: The selected widget appears on the user’s home screen, allowing direct access to data without opening the app.

Title: Verify Minimum Device Requirements
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android device with API 24 or higher, 1GB RAM, 300MB free storage, GPS enabled
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a device meeting the specified requirements.
2. Launch the application.
3. Perform basic operations (e.g., open a widget or check notifications).
Expected Result: The application launches and operates without errors or performance issues.

Title: Verify Continuous Internet Connection Requirement
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Device with stable internet connection, device without internet connection
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application on a device with a stable internet connection.
2. Perform operations requiring data access (e.g., fetch notifications or widget data).
3. Disconnect the internet and attempt the same operations.
Expected Result: Application functions correctly with internet; operations fail or show errors without internet.
